title: Distribution / Host Integration SSOT

loop.observation: C11 distribution/host knowledge is already generated from manifests and source checks, but child page manifests still appeared as standalone loop debt.
loop.hypothesis: The distribution-host root can own the evidence loop for the C11 surface while index fragments delegate to the generated root reader or their narrower sub-reader.
loop.execute: Generate the C11 distribution-host reader, verify source checks across hostintegration, policy, packaging, local API, and roadmap anchors, and publish distribution-host evidence.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when required pages, source anchors, assertions, or generated reader freshness drift away from the manifest.
loop.retrospective: C11 remains a small public surface: reviewers inspect the root reader and only open detail fragments when a specific host concept changes.

summary: C11 Distribution / Host Integration owns distribution channel identity, Store/App helper boundaries, local IPC facts, Store review/demo mode, and host-facing privacy metadata.
Reader documentation is generated from this manifest while detailed policy tables and metadata allowlists remain generated by their dedicated evidence tools.

source_checks.name: store-policy-input
source_checks.contains: OSGrantPresent means the caller has already reduced

source_checks.name: mac-provider-cli-review
source_checks.contains: mac-app-store provider CLI path requires a sandbox

source_checks.name: msix-store-service-forbidden
source_checks.contains: Windows service install is forbidden by default for msix-store

source_checks.name: review-demo-offline-test
source_checks.contains: review demo mode must not allow provider execution

assertions: C11 owns host distribution facts, but does not own provider process execution or SaaS assignment state.
Store channel policy decisions are generated from executable policy code and store contract inputs.
Review/demo mode is offline, local-only, and cannot enable provider execution or telemetry sync.
Q-DIST-001 resolved / Q-DIST-001 RESOLVED: Mac App Store external provider CLI execution requires user-selected or sandbox/security-scoped OS grant plus Store review approval.
Q-CTX-005 Store App repository / adapter ownership is closed in C11/store architecture docs; future desktop/app repository remains outside this public daemon repository.
Store App GUI must remain a client of C11/local API contracts and provider process 를 직접 spawn 하지 않는다.
Store App distribution surfaces provider CLI 를 bundle / download / silently install 하지 않는다.
Only unresolved store-channel questions remain in the roadmap; resolved decisions move into generated domain docs.
